risk_title: Conflict-driven displacement underrepresentation in project benefits
risk_summary: Displaced persons are receiving disproportionately fewer benefits from recovery activities than targeted. Only 38,192 displaced beneficiaries have accessed climate-resilient infrastructure against a year 2 target of 196,000, indicating a significant implementation gap in reaching conflict-displaced populations.
evidence_quote: The year 2 target is 196,000, and this indicator is behind the target. The MTR will be utilized to assess the reason why less than expected number of displaced persons are benefitting from the recovery activities.

risk_title: Low community recovery plan implementation in conflict-affected areas
risk_summary: Community recovery plans are being implemented at only 9% (52 out of 576 kebeles) against a year 2 target of 40%, suggesting weak institutional capacity or ongoing conflict dynamics preventing plan execution in conflict-affected communities.
evidence_quote: 52 out of 576 project supported kebeles have their recovery plans implemented. This is less than the year 2 target of 40%.

risk_title: Neighborhood Relations Committees functionality uncertain in conflict context
risk_summary: Neighborhood Relations Committees (NRCs) formed for conflict mitigation have not yet demonstrated sustained functionality. Communities where NRCs were established have only recently begun receiving funding, raising questions about their ability to maintain operations and prevent community tensions in ongoing conflict-affected areas.
evidence_quote: NRCs have been formed. Communities where NRCs have been formed only started receiving funding less than one year ago. The next ISR will provide more details on the NRC's functionality.

risk_title: Persistent high overall risk rating despite satisfactory implementation progress
risk_summary: The project maintains a 'High' overall risk rating across multiple categories including Political and Governance, Macroeconomic, Fiduciary, and Environment and Social risks, indicating that underlying FCV vulnerabilities continue to threaten project sustainability despite current satisfactory implementation progress.
evidence_quote: Risk Category: Political and Governance [High], Macroeconomic [High], Fiduciary [High], Environment and Social [High], Other [High]
